title: sorbus aria var. cretica
intro: Sorbus aria var. cretica, commonly known as Cretan whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Sorbus aria var. cretica have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and rheumatism, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herbal preparations of Sorbus aria var. cretica include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and internally to alleviate pain and reduce fever.
